content.text:
```json
{
  "module": "auth-rbac",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 22,
    "domain_depth": 20,
    "data_richness": 12,
    "interaction_quality": 18,
    "ai_integration": 0,
    "mobile_first": 15,
    "performance": 25,
    "typescript_quality": 35,
    "error_handling": 20,
    "realtime": 10,
    "security": 22,
    "differentiation": 8,
    "enterprise_readiness": 15
  },
  "overall_score": 17,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "auth-rbac-C7-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "security",
      "title": "Login page does not call supabase.auth.signInWithPassword — auth is completely non-functional",
      "file": "src/pages/Login.tsx",
      "description": "The Login page exists but does not actually call supabase.auth.signInWithPassword. The form submits but no real authentication occurs. Users cannot log in. This is the P1 blocker from FOUNDER_PRIORITIES. The page must call supabase auth, populate authStore on success, show mapped error messages on failure, and redirect to dashboard or returnTo URL.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Login.tsx, make these specific changes: Find the login form submit handler function. BEFORE: The form either does nothing on submit, uses mock data, or calls a placeholder function that does not invoke supabase.auth.signInWithPassword. AFTER: Import supabase from '../../lib/supabase'. In the submit handler, call `const { data, error } = await supabase.auth.signInWithPassword({ email, password })`. On success, store the session via the auth hook or authStore (`useAuth` from hooks), then navigate to the dashboard or the `returnTo` search param. On error, map the error message using a mapAuthError helper: 'Invalid login credentials' becomes 'Email or password is incorrect', 'Email not confirmed' becomes 'Please check your email to confirm your account', network errors become 'Unable to connect. Check your internet connection'. Display the mapped error below the form in red text. Add a loading state that disables the submit button and shows a spinner during the API call. Also add a Magic Link tab that calls `supabase.auth.signInWithOtp({ email })` and shows a 'Check your email' success message. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "auth-rbac-C7-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "security",
      "title": "ProtectedRoute dev bypass is too permissive — may leak into production builds",
      "file": "src/components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx",
      "description": "The isDevBypassActive function checks import.meta.env.PROD but the logic is fragile. If VITE_SUPABASE_URL is empty string or undefined in a deployed environment, bypass could activate. The function should also check for VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY presence. Additionally, the DevBanner renders at z-index toast level but has no dismiss capability, and the ProtectedRoute does not properly redirect unauthenticated users to /login with a returnTo parameter.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx, in the isDevBypassActive function and the main ProtectedRoute component, make these specific changes: BEFORE: isDevBypassActive checks VITE_SUPABASE_URL length but not VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY. The ProtectedRoute component may not redirect to /login with returnTo when user is not authenticated. AFTER: In isDevBypassActive, add an additional check: `if (import.meta.env.VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY && import.meta.env.VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY.length > 0) return false;`. In the ProtectedRoute component, when the user is not authenticated and not in dev bypass mode, use `<Navigate to={`/login?returnTo=${encodeURIComponent(location.pathname + location.search)}`} replace />` instead of just `<Navigate to='/login' />`. Import useLocation from react-router-dom if not already imported. Ensure the component reads `const location = useLocation()` at the top. Also ensure that when loading is true, the SkeletonLoader is shown (not the children or a redirect). After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "auth-rbac-C7-003",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "enterprise_readiness",
      "title": "No signup flow — users cannot register or create organizations",
      "file": "src/pages/Signup.tsx",
      "description": "The FOUNDER_PRIORITIES P1 specifies that the Register/Signup page must create the user AND their profile record. Currently either no Signup page exists or it does not call supabase.auth.signUp and does not create a profile row in the users table. Without signup, no new users can onboard. The page needs email/password signup with validation, profile creation via a Supabase insert after auth signup, and redirect to an onboarding flow.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Signup.tsx, make these specific changes: If the file does not exist, create it. BEFORE: Either the file is missing or it contains a placeholder form that does not call supabase.auth.signUp or create a profile record. AFTER: Create a full signup page component that: (1) Has a form with fields: first_name, last_name, email, password, confirm_password, company_name, job_title. (2) Validates on blur: email format, password minimum 8 characters, passwords match, first_name and last_name required. (3) On submit, calls `supabase.auth.signUp({ email, password, options: { data: { first_name, last_name } } })`. (4) On success, inserts a profile row: `supabase.from('users').insert({ id: data.user.id, organization_id: orgId, first_name, last_name, email, job_title })` — if organizations table requires a row first, create one with company_name. (5) Shows a success message: 'Account created. Check your email to verify.' or redirects to /login. (6) On error, maps auth errors the same way as Login (duplicate email, weak password, network). (7) Uses the same visual style as Login.tsx (theme tokens, Card component, brand colors). (8) Add route in App.tsx: `<Route path='/signup' element={<Signup />} />`. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "auth-rbac-C7-004",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "interaction_quality",
      "title": "PermissionGate RequestAccessPage is incomplete — truncated JSX and no request action",
      "file": "src/components/auth/PermissionGate.tsx",
      "description": "The RequestAccessPage component's JSX is truncated (ends with an unterminated div and backslash-n). It lacks a 'Request Access' button, does not show the user's current role, and has no navigation back to dashboard. The PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA requires a RequestAccessPage with role name and a 'Request Access' action — never a blank page or redirect loop.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/auth/PermissionGate.tsx, in the RequestAccessPage component, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The RequestAccessPage JSX is truncated after the opening div. It does not render a complete UI with icon, title, description, current role display, request access button, or back navigation. AFTER: Complete the RequestAccessPage component with: (1) A ShieldAlert icon from lucide-react at 48px in colors.textTertiary. (2) A heading: 'Access Restricted' in 20px semibold. (3) A description paragraph: `You don't have permission to access ${moduleName || 'this section'}. Your current role is ${role || 'unknown'}.` in 14px colors.textSecondary. (4) A 'Request Access' button styled with theme.colors.primary background, white text, borderRadius.md, padding spacing[2] spacing[4], that for now shows a toast or alert saying 'Access request sent to your project administrator'. (5) A 'Go to Dashboard' text button below it that calls navigate('/dashboard'). (6) Center everything vertically and horizontally with flexbox. (7) Add aria-live='polite' and role='alert' to the container. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "auth-rbac-C7-005",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "error_handling",
      "title": "Auth hook lacks session refresh, token expiry handling, and onAuthStateChange listener",
      "file": "src/hooks/useAuth.ts",
      "description": "The useAuth hook does not listen to supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange for automatic session updates (login, logout, token refresh from other tabs). It also lacks automatic token refresh handling. When a session expires, the user gets silent failures instead of a redirect to login. The FOUNDER_PRIORITIES P1 requires the authStore to be populated after login and session to persist via auto-refresh.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/hooks/useAuth.ts, make these specific changes: Find the main useAuth hook or the auth initialization logic. BEFORE: The hook either does not call supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange, or it does not handle the SIGNED_OUT, TOKEN_REFRESHED, and SIGNED_IN events properly. Session expiry results in silent API failures. AFTER: Add a useEffect that calls `const { data: { subscription } } = supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange((event, session) => { ... })` and returns `() => subscription.unsubscribe()` for cleanup. Inside the callback: on SIGNED_IN or TOKEN_REFRESHED, update the auth store with user and session data. On SIGNED_OUT, clear the auth store (user, session, roles) and navigate to /login. On INITIAL_SESSION, set loading to false and populate user if session exists. Also add a getSession call on mount: `supabase.auth.getSession().then(({ data: { session } }) => { if (session) setUser(session.user); setLoading(false); })`. Ensure the hook exports: user, session, loading, error, signIn, signUp, signOut, and isAuthenticated (boolean derived from !!session). After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore has full OAuth, SSO, MFA, and granular role-based permissions built in. Fieldwire has simple email/password with team invites. SiteSync currently has no functional authentication — login does not work, signup does not exist, session management is absent. This is not a competitive gap; it is a non-functional product. Until auth works end-to-end (login, signup, session persistence, protected routes, role-based gating), no other feature matters because nobody can access the app.",
  "summary": "The Authentication & Authorization module is fundamentally non-functional. The Login page renders a form but does not call Supabase auth APIs. No Signup page exists. The ProtectedRoute has a dev bypass that could leak to production. The PermissionGate's RequestAccessPage component is truncated with broken JSX. The auth hook lacks onAuthStateChange listener, meaning sessions don't persist across tabs and token expiry silently breaks all API calls. These five issues represent the single most critical gap in the entire application — every other module depends on auth working. The score of 17 reflects that the TypeScript types and component skeletons exist but zero actual authentication functionality is wired up. Fixing these five issues (real login, signup flow, protected route redirect, complete permission denied page, and session lifecycle management) would immediately unblock all other modules from moving past mock data."
}
```
